name: Railway
philosophy: An inspired interpretation of Railway's design language — an infrastructure-deployment brand built on a deep midnight-violet (#13111c) canvas, monospace-heavy chrome, glowing purple/lavender voltage at button moments, and a graph-of-services hero that mirrors the actual product (deployments arranged as a node graph). The brand reads as 'the magical version of cloud' — technical, but slightly playful.

design_language.motion_signature: Signature is the service-graph nodes pulsing on deploy (~1.5s breathing cycle with a 8% scale variation); link hovers fade at 200ms; deploy-progress bars sweep at 400ms ease-out.

trademark_signals: Midnight-violet canvas (#13111c) — deliberately not black, deliberately not navy, a violet-tinted near-black that IS the brand chrome
Service graph as hero — the homepage shows a node graph of database, API, cron, worker, all connected with hairlines and pulsing with deploy activity
Lavender purple accent (#9c7dff) on CTAs with a subtle outer glow — the brand's voltage moment, never pure white
Berkeley Mono everywhere — service names, environment variables, deployment URLs all render in monospace, not Inter — the mono-heavy chrome IS the brand signature
Hairlines drawn in low-alpha lavender (rgba(196,181,253,0.12)) instead of gray — even the dividers carry the brand color
ai_slop_avoided: Don't paint the canvas pure black or navy — the specific midnight-violet (#13111c) IS the brand
Don't substitute the accent with cyan, blue, or pink — the lavender purple (#9c7dff) is the only accent
Don't drop the mono chrome. Service names, env vars, deployment URLs are all monospace — this is the brand's tonal signature
Don't use a static product screenshot for the hero — the service graph is interactive and pulses with the deploy state
Don't add a second voltage moment. The lavender CTA is the only saturated color in the system
